Title: Chloroplast competition is controlled by lipid biosynthesis in evening primroses
Journal: Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences of the United States of America

Common_Genes.Source_Species_Variety: I-johSt; II-suavG; IV-atroSt and related plastomes analyzed
Common_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: johansen Standard nuclear background; plastome I/III variants and WT plastomes
Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Large plastid ORF of unknown function; shares weak homology to FtsH protease/AAA protein family
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Approximately 7 kb transcript detected; lower processed/degradation bands differed between strong plastome I and weak plastome IV
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Transcript pattern differed between strong WT plastome I and weak WT plastome IV, with weak plastome IV resembling weak plastome I variants in lower bands
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Likely affects envelope lipid composition that influences plastid division/turnover and chloroplast competition
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Biparental plastid transmission assays; plastome mutator-derived variants; isolated chloroplast ACCase assay; seedling lipid profiling
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Correlation mapping of WT and mutagenized plastomes; inheritance crosses; transcript blot analysis; ACCase activity comparison in lines with ycf2 mutations but no accD mutation
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Polymorphisms in ycf2 alter chloroplast inheritance strength and influence ACCase activity, likely indirectly affecting lipid biosynthesis and plastid competitiveness
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ycf2 polymorphisms, especially in promoter/5'-UTR and a mutated coding-site region, strongly correlated with inheritance strength. 2. Very weak plastome III variants carried prominent ycf2 mutations, supporting causal involvement. 3. ycf2 variation affected ACCase activity even in backgrounds without accD mutation, indicating a functional link to lipid biosynthesis. 4. ycf2 transcript processing/accumulation patterns differed between strong and weak plastomes.
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Function remains unknown, so mechanistic manipulation may have unpredictable pleiotropic effects on chloroplast physiology and inheritance
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential plastome target for controlling chloroplast competitiveness and transmission, but application is limited by unknown biochemical function and possible broad plastid effects
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Authors classify ycf2 as a major candidate causal locus, whereas oriB and ycf1 were not supported by mutant analyses; proposed role may involve regulation of lipid metabolism rather than DNA replication

Pathway_Genes.Source_Species_Variety: I-johSt; II-suavG; IV-atroSt and related plastomes analyzed
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: johansen Standard nuclear background; plastomes I-johSt, II-suavG, IV-atroSt and plastome I/III variants
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Plastid-encoded β-carboxyltransferase subunit of heteromeric acetyl-CoA carboxylase; highly variable N terminus with glutamic acid-rich repeat region
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Initial committed step of plastid fatty acid synthesis supplying membrane lipid biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Catalyzes the first, tightly regulated, rate-limiting step of fatty acid biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Functions as the plastid ACCase β-carboxyltransferase subunit in conversion of acetyl-CoA and bicarbonate into malonyl-CoA during the first step of fatty acid synthesis
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Envelope lipid composition influences chloroplast division and/or turnover and thereby plastid competitiveness during biparental inheritance
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Weak plastome IV appeared to overexpress accD relative to strong plastome I by Northern blot
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: accD transcript abundance differed among plastomes; weak plastome IV showed higher transcript level than strong plastome I, but weakened plastome I variants did not consistently show overexpression
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Biparental plastid transmission assays in constant johansen Standard nuclear background; seedling lipid profiling; isolated chloroplast ACCase assays
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Correlation mapping of plastomes; plastome mutator-derived variants; chloroplast inheritance crosses; Northern blot; ACCase enzyme activity assay; lipidomics with LASSO prediction
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Polymorphisms in the accD promoter/5'-UTR and N terminus alter ACCase activity and lipid composition, thereby changing chloroplast competitive strength/inheritance behavior
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Sequence divergence in the accD regulatory region and 5' coding region showed strong correlation with plastid inheritance strength in wild-type plastomes. 2. Independent plastome mutator variants confirmed accD as a causal locus affecting competitive ability. 3. Mutations/polymorphisms in the accD N terminus co-occurred with increased ACCase activity. 4. Altered accD states were linked to changes in lipid profiles predictive of chloroplast competitiveness.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Suggests plastid-encoded lipid biosynthesis genes can control organelle inheritance and cytonuclear behavior; useful for plastome engineering or managing biparental plastid transmission, but may unpredictably alter envelope lipid composition and organelle competition
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Changes affect chloroplast inheritance/competition and lipid metabolism; simple relationship between ACCase activity and competitiveness was not established
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The causal polymorphisms largely comprise in-frame indels in repetitive regions spanning promoter, 5'-UTR, and N terminus; effects are metabolic rather than due to ptDNA copy number or replication origin variation
Pathway_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: ACCase alpha-carboxyltransferase; biotin-carboxyl carrier protein; biotin carboxylase; ycf2 (functional influence inferred)
